mission.title: kubevirt: Specify the service accounts mounted into the VM
mission.description: Specify the service accounts mounted into the VM. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current kubevirt deployment
steps.description:
Verify your virtctl version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
helm list -n kubevirt 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubevirt installation.

steps.title: Review kubevirt configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubevirt configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl get configmap -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevirt
```
I want to mount an EKS service account containing an IAM role.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Specify the service accounts mounted into the VM
steps.description:
Thanks alot for the detailed description
Trying to make sense of your solution, so i will rephrase what i understand and please correct me if i am wrong. First of all..

> It would check to see if a service account is mounted as volume.

How might this look ? I envision something like this (in the
```yaml
Environment:
    # SOME AWS ENV VARIABLES HERE
Mounts:
    /var/run/secrets/eks.amazonaws.com/serviceaccount from aws-iam-token (ro)
    /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount from kube-api-access-j8cqt (ro)
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl get events -n kubevirt --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Specify the service accounts mounted into the VM" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Thanks alot for the detailed description
Trying to make sense of your solution, so i will rephrase what i understand and please correct me if i am wrong. First of all..

> It would check to see if a service account is mounted as volume.
codeSnippets:
Environment:
    # SOME AWS ENV VARIABLES HERE
Mounts:
    /var/run/secrets/eks.amazonaws.com/serviceaccount from aws-iam-token (ro)
    /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount from kube-api-access-j8cqt (ro)

  spec:

    template:

      spec:
        architecture: amd64
        domain:
          cpu:
            cores: 8
            dedicatedCpuPlacement: true
          devices:
            filesystems:
            - name: service-account
              virtiofs: {}
          firmware: {}
          machine:
            type: q35
          mem

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubevirt installed or the issue environment reproducible.
